87200

November 17, 1976

David E. Kessinger
3240 Pachappa Hill
Riverside, Calif. 92506

Dear Mr. Kessinger:

This is in response to your telephone inquiry
to Gina Kutchins regarding your reporting obligations
under the Political Reform Act, and my subsequent telep
phone conversation with you.

The question you presented was whether you
are required to report income totaling $250 or more
that you receive from outside the local jurisdiction.
You specifically mentioned unemployment insurance bene-
fits, supplemental grants for basic education and Medi-
Cal benefits. You indicated that you file financial
disclosure statements in your capacity as a member of
the Riverside County Democratic Central Committee.

As a member of the Riverside County Democratic
Central Committee, you are not required under the Poli-
tical Reform Act to file financial disclosure statements.
For a definition of who must file financial disclosure
statements, please refer to §87200 of the Government
Code. (A copy of the Political Reform Act of 1974, as
amended is enclosed.) If you hold some other position
which is included in the definition of who must file,
please send us a letter so indicating, andtthe staff will
address the question in light of that information.

Sincerely,


Jeanne Pritchard
Administrative Assistant
Executive Division

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

November 17, 1976

David E. Kessinger
2240 Pachappa Hill
Riverside, Calif. 92506

Dear Mr. Kessinger:

This is in response to your telephone inquiry
to Gina Kutchins regarding your reporting obligations
under the Political Reform Act, and my subsequent tele-
phone conversation with you.

The question you presented was whether you
are required to report income totaling $250 or more
that you receive from outside the local jurisdiction.
You specifically mentioned unemployment insurance bene-
fits, supplemental grants for basic education and Medi-
cal benefits. You indicated that you file financial
disclosure statements in your capacity as a member of
the Riverside County Democratic Central Committee.

As a member of the Riverside County Democratic
Central Committee, you are not required under the Poli-
tical Reform Act to file financial disclosure statements.
For a definition of who must file financial disclosure
statements, please refer to §87200 of the Government
Code. (A copy of the Political Reform Act of 1974, as
Amended is enclosed.) If you hold some other position
which is included in the definition of who must file,
please send us a letter so indicating, and the staff will
address the question in light of that information.

[illegible handwritten text]

Sincerely,

Jeanne Pritchard
Administrative Assistant
Executive Division

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441


[illegible]


FROM: Jeanne

DATE: October 27, 1976

RE: Telephone request for advice from David E. Kessinger
3240 Pachappa Hill, Riverside 92506
(714) 686-2138


Are unemployment insurance benefits,
supplemental grants for basic education
and Social Security benefits received by
an official reportable as "income"?


State of California

Memorandum

To      :  Jeanne Pritchard                          Date   :  October 26, 1976


From :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        GINA

Subject:  David E. Kessinger, 3240 Pachappa Hill, Riverside, 92506,
          714/696-2138
          696-2138


The above mentioned individual stated that he requested an
opinion from the Commission a year ago and has never
received a response and wants to know why.  He stated he
sent the request on a postcard.  Ring a bell?

I think I have asked you about him before and you stated
you did not have anything in the file.  Maybe we should
contact him and ask him what the question was.

[illegible]

[illegible]
